column1: 2004-07-27‚ÄéToday I got up around 10:00 after sleeping in for the first time. Boy did that feel good. Then I hung around all day doing nothing
column2: but playing games on the computer and talking ot friends
column3: before leaving to go to the RSS Grand Rapids
column4: Marine Corps station and working out and running. We actually played a soccer game. However
column5: I hurt my SSGT Palmer in the last minute of the game. I felt really bad. He could have fractured his bone below his knee cap. Yikes!! I didn't mean to do it and he knows that and he tells me not to feel sorry
column6: but I do and I don't know what to do. I'm sorry SSGT Plamer. Please for give me. For now I have nothing more to say....but I'll write more later.  ---Gyzmo

column1: 2004-07-25‚Äé"So today we head home from Battle Creek after a 5 mile ""moto run"" (motivational run) and morning chow. Boy am I tired
column2: but I'm not complaning yet. I like it here and I wish that I didn't have to leave. Oh well though. It will be good to go home and see some friends and of course my family. Gotta go. Bye!  ---Gyzmo"

column3: Michigan around 5:30 Pmand experiance some tought drills
column4: and training. I thought it was fun though. Then around 8:00 we headed into the mess hall for chow and we had our first meal and lesson about chow time. Then we headed back to thebarracks for tivoli. Then we hit the dubbies and the head
column6: after a debriefing about Fire watch for each of the 21 females from 4 different RSS platoons in Mchigan. Then it bagan. Fire watch and I had the first duty. That was quiet interesting. Then the next day revoli was at 5:30 am and it was off to the guys barracks and then the Chow hall for some morning chow. And man did we PT a lot because our guidon had been stolen in the guys barracks
column7: because they sucked a fire watch. Anyways
column11: and games in the boondocks and classes about Marine Corps basic knowledge and history. It was fun
column12: and I was tired though and I lost my voice. It was hard that night to do fire watch.  But I'll tell you more on this later when I get the time to write
column13: for now though I have to go. Bye!  ---Gyzmo

column1: 2004-07-23‚ÄéSo this morning I am packing a backpack with clothes for a weekend trip to Fort Custer
column3: Michigan.....where I will be training wiht the United States Marine Corps for boot camp (basic training). It should be fun and I am excited
column4: and nervous to go. I am to leave at about 1:00 pm for the Fort and I won't be home until late Sunday night. See you all later!  ---Gyzmo

column1: 2004-07-14‚ÄéHe's alive.....I'll tell ya the story later!!!!!!  Thank God for Pat.  Sorry God
column2: I never meant to be mean and say that I hated you.....I was just angry but I bet you already new that
column3: because you have my whole life already planned out.  Thanks God for bringing Pat back to our lives.  And I'd like to pray for Nick who was also in the car accident and did die.  Please God be with his family.  And I pray that his soul would be at rest.  He was my special project and I did my best to preach the word of God to him
column4: in every way. And I pray that Pat will come from his surgery and all will be alright.  ---Gyzmo

column3: because today is Pat's visitations at his funeral.  That's right a funneral for a seventeen-year old.  God
column6: and sad by this whole thing.  I don't even know what to say or think right now.  It's not fair!  Where's God?????  Where the heck is God at a time like this and how could he let something like this happen?  Well
column7: you could say that God wanted him home and that's why he had to die
column8: well there's been some times when I've wanted things and I haven't gotten them.  Why should God!?!?!  I hate God! I hate him!!!!

column2: and he was only 17 years old.....but ya know what gets me the most is......Where is God?  I'm so confused by what happened

column2: this site was created today for anyone
column4: to read and just get a grip on what a teenager might have to say about life. Many teens might have thoughts or questions about life
